finding: ATM-GOV-0234 exposed a governed batch deadlock: batch checkpoint refused stale frozen runner writes, runner-sync build required a sealed HEAD commit, source-first checkpoint partially closed live ledger before refusing, terminal tasks could not enqueue runner-sync, and the broker kept a stale ATM-GOV-0234 write intent that blocked ATM-GOV-0235 shared-surface claim with ATM_BROKER_SHARED_QUEUE_BLOCKED.
expectedBehavior: Batch queue-head delivery should expose one executable broker-ticket recovery path: either checkpoint can reserve a runner-sync/build ticket for staged queue-head source, or ATM provides a safe pre-checkpoint delivery commit lane and then auto-releases task broker intents during lock cleanup. Source-first refusal must not leave partial close side effects.
evidenceOrRepro: During ATM-GOV-0234 on 2026-07-21, `node atm.mjs batch checkpoint --actor codex-plan3-captain-20260721-02 --json` returned ATM_RUNNER_STALE_WRITE_REFUSED; `ATM_RETAIN_RELEASE_ARTIFACTS=1 npm run build` required ATM_RUNNER_SYNC_QUEUE_HEAD_REQUIRED; `node atm.dev.mjs batch checkpoint` later returned ATM_SOURCE_FIRST_WRITE_REFUSED after live-ledger close effects; terminal ATM-GOV-0234 then rejected runner-sync enqueue with ATM_RUNNER_SYNC_ENQUEUE_TASK_INVALID, and ATM-GOV-0235 claim failed until `node atm.mjs broker release --task ATM-GOV-0234 --json` cleared the stale intent.
followUp: Implement transactional batch runner-sync recovery with exactly-once close side effects, auto broker-intent cleanup on terminal lock cleanup, and a registered recovery command for ATM_BROKER_SHARED_QUEUE_BLOCKED that returns a broker ticket/status instead of a bare refusal. Until that lands, ATM 3.0 final closure must treat this item as a blocker rather than a waiver; the 2026-07-21 evidence-repair pass only hardened replay/final-verdict tests so fixture evidence cannot falsely close the plan.
